ucs.title: [LLM] FIN4 Outlook inbox rules hiding phishing/security keywords (mailbox stealth)
ucs.description: Hunts for newly created Exchange/OWA inbox rules that auto-delete or move-to-RSS-Feeds/Deleted-Items any message whose body or subject contains FIN4's signature keyword set (hacked, phish, phishing, malware, suspicious, fraud, virus). FIN4 is famous for logging into compromised C-suite/IR/legal mailboxes and planting these rules so that the real user never sees the helpdesk's warning replies, allowing FIN4 to keep harvesting M&A/insider correspondence without alerting the victim.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(All_Changes.command) as command values(All_Changes.src) as src_ip values(All_Changes.user_agent) as ua from datamodel=Change where (All_Changes.action=created OR All_Changes.action=modified) (All_Changes.command="*New-InboxRule*" OR All_Changes.command="*Set-InboxRule*" OR All_Changes.command="*UpdateInboxRules*") (All_Changes.command="*hack*" OR All_Changes.command="*phish*" OR All_Changes.command="*malware*" OR All_Changes.command="*suspicious*" OR All_Changes.command="*fraud*" OR All_Changes.command="*virus*" OR All_Changes.command="*RSS Feeds*" OR All_Changes.command="*MoveToFolder*Deleted*" OR All_Changes.command="*MarkAsRead*") by All_Changes.user All_Changes.dest | `drop_dm_object_name("All_Changes")` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
CloudAppEvents
| where Application in ("Microsoft Exchange Online","Office 365")
| where ActionType in ("New-InboxRule","Set-InboxRule","UpdateInboxRules","Update-InboxRules")
| extend Params = tostring(RawEventData.Parameters)
| where Params has_any ("hack","phish","malware","suspicious","fraud","virus","helpdesk","security")
   or Params has_any ("RSS Feeds","Deleted Items","Junk Email","MarkAsRead","DeleteMessage")
| project Timestamp, AccountDisplayName, AccountObjectId, IPAddress, UserAgent, ActionType, Params, ISP, CountryCode
| join kind=leftouter (AADSignInEventsBeta | project AccountObjectId, SessionId, IPAddress, RiskLevelDuringSignIn) on AccountObjectId, IPAddress
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: This is FIN4's most uniquely attributable behaviour - documented by FireEye/Mandiant in 'Hacking the Street' (2014). Unlike most APTs, FIN4 deploys NO malware on the endpoint; their persistence IS the mailbox rule. Generic detection of inbox-rule creation has high FP, but the keyword set (hacked/phish/malware) plus the move-to-RSS-Feeds folder is a near-pathognomonic FIN4 signature and is rare in

ucs.title: [LLM] FIN4 OWA/EWS credential reuse from Tor exit nodes after Office macro lure
ucs.description: Correlates two FIN4-specific events: (1) a user opening a weaponized Office document that spawns a VBA-driven fake Outlook/Windows credential prompt (T1059.005 + T1056.002), and (2) within 24h a successful interactive sign-in to that same identity from a Tor exit IP (T1090.003) hitting Exchange/OWA. FIN4 historically uses macro-based fake login dialogs to harvest creds, then immediately validates them by logging into OWA over Tor to read M&A communications.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe")) (Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe")) by Processes.user Processes.dest Processes.parent_process Processes.process _time | `drop_dm_object_name("Processes")` | rename user as victim_user | join type=inner victim_user [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.app IN ("owa","exchange","office365","AzureActiveDirectory") Authentication.action=success by Authentication.user Authentication.src Authentication.app _time | `drop_dm_object_name("Authentication")` | rename user as victim_user | lookup tor_exit_nodes ip as src OUTPUT is_tor | where is_tor="true"] | where lastTime_auth > firstTime_proc AND lastTime_auth-firstTime_proc < 86400 | table victim_user dest parent_process process src app
ucs.kql:
let macroVictims = DeviceProcessEvents
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe")
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe")
   or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("CredentialPicker","PromptForCredential","Get-Credential","Outlook","OWA login")
| project MacroTime=Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountUpn=tostring(strcat(AccountName,"@",AccountDomain)), InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine;
let torLogons = AADSignInEventsBeta
| where ErrorCode == 0
| where Application has_any ("Office 365","Exchange","Outlook Web","OWA")
| where NetworkLocationDetails has "tor" or IsAnonymousProxy == true or RiskLevelDuringSignIn in ("high","medium")
| project LogonTime=Timestamp, AccountUpn, IPAddress, Application, Country, UserAgent, SessionId;
macroVictims
| join kind=inner torLogons on AccountUpn
| where LogonTime between (MacroTime .. MacroTime + 24h)
| project MacroTime, LogonTime, AccountUpn, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, IPAddress, Country, Application, UserAgent
| order by LogonTime desc
ucs.rationale: This chain mirrors the exact FIN4 playbook documented by FireEye: targeted spearphish to investor-relations / legal / C-suite at pharma & healthcare firms, VBA macro popping a fake Outlook credential dialog (T1056.002), then Tor-fronted OWA logon within hours to read deal correspondence. The combination of (Office-spawned scripting host) + (same user's successful M365 logon from Tor) within 24h is
